verses.text: The word against Babylon: 'Babylon is taken, Bel is confounded'; a nation from the north shall make her desolate. The mighty empire falls. The rod God used to chasten His people is itself broken at last.

The word against Babylon: 'Babylon is taken, Bel is confounded'; a nation from the north shall make her desolate. The mighty empire falls. The rod God used to chasten His people is itself broken at last.

The word against Babylon: 'Babylon is taken, Bel is confounded'; a nation from the north shall make her desolate. The mighty empire falls. The rod God used to chasten His people is itself broken at last.

In those days Israel and Judah shall come weeping, seeking the LORD, joined to Him in 'a perpetual covenant that shall not be forgotten.' Affliction turns them home. God uses Babylon's fall to draw His people back to Himself.

In those days Israel and Judah shall come weeping, seeking the LORD, joined to Him in 'a perpetual covenant that shall not be forgotten.' Affliction turns them home. God uses Babylon's fall to draw His people back to Himself.

In those days Israel and Judah shall come weeping, seeking the LORD, joined to Him in 'a perpetual covenant that shall not be forgotten.' Affliction turns them home. God uses Babylon's fall to draw His people back to Himself.

In those days Israel and Judah shall come weeping, seeking the LORD, joined to Him in 'a perpetual covenant that shall not be forgotten.' Affliction turns them home. God uses Babylon's fall to draw His people back to Himself.
'Remove out of the midst of Babylon'; God raises northern nations against her, and she becomes a spoil. His people are called out. We are to flee the doomed city of sin lest we share its ruin.
'Remove out of the midst of Babylon'; God raises northern nations against her, and she becomes a spoil. His people are called out. We are to flee the doomed city of sin lest we share its ruin.
'Remove out of the midst of Babylon'; God raises northern nations against her, and she becomes a spoil. His people are called out. We are to flee the doomed city of sin lest we share its ruin.
Though they rejoiced in plundering His heritage, Babylon shall be wholly desolate, an astonishment to all who pass by. Their joy turns to horror. Those who triumph over God's people answer for it.
Though they rejoiced in plundering His heritage, Babylon shall be wholly desolate, an astonishment to all who pass by. Their joy turns to horror. Those who triumph over God's people answer for it.
Though they rejoiced in plundering His heritage, Babylon shall be wholly desolate, an astonishment to all who pass by. Their joy turns to horror. Those who triumph over God's people answer for it.
Set yourselves against Babylon, for 'she hath sinned against the LORD'; cut off sower and reaper alike. Her sin is the charge. Every nation is judged at last by its standing before God.
Set yourselves against Babylon, for 'she hath sinned against the LORD'; cut off sower and reaper alike. Her sin is the charge. Every nation is judged at last by its standing before God.
Set yourselves against Babylon, for 'she hath sinned against the LORD'; cut off sower and reaper alike. Her sin is the charge. Every nation is judged at last by its standing before God.

Israel was a scattered sheep, torn by lions; God will punish Babylon as He did Assyria, and Israel's iniquity, when sought, shall not be found, for He will pardon. Sin is blotted out. The God who chastens also forgives His own.

Israel was a scattered sheep, torn by lions; God will punish Babylon as He did Assyria, and Israel's iniquity, when sought, shall not be found, for He will pardon. Sin is blotted out. The God who chastens also forgives His own.

Israel was a scattered sheep, torn by lions; God will punish Babylon as He did Assyria, and Israel's iniquity, when sought, shall not be found, for He will pardon. Sin is blotted out. The God who chastens also forgives His own.
Go up against the rebel land; 'how is the hammer of the whole earth cut asunder!' Babylon, who smote the nations, is herself shattered. The instrument of God's wrath is not exempt from it.
Go up against the rebel land; 'how is the hammer of the whole earth cut asunder!' Babylon, who smote the nations, is herself shattered. The instrument of God's wrath is not exempt from it.
Go up against the rebel land; 'how is the hammer of the whole earth cut asunder!' Babylon, who smote the nations, is herself shattered. The instrument of God's wrath is not exempt from it.

'I have laid a snare for thee... O Babylon... thou hast striven against the LORD.' Her warriors are given to slaughter. Pride against God ensnares. To contend with the Almighty is to be trapped by Him.

'I have laid a snare for thee... O Babylon... thou hast striven against the LORD.' Her warriors are given to slaughter. Pride against God ensnares. To contend with the Almighty is to be trapped by Him.

'I have laid a snare for thee... O Babylon... thou hast striven against the LORD.' Her warriors are given to slaughter. Pride against God ensnares. To contend with the Almighty is to be trapped by Him.

'I have laid a snare for thee... O Babylon... thou hast striven against the LORD.' Her warriors are given to slaughter. Pride against God ensnares. To contend with the Almighty is to be trapped by Him.
Fugitives carry to Zion the news of 'the vengeance of the LORD... for his temple'; her young men fall in the streets. God avenges His house. The desecration of His worship is repaid in full.
Fugitives carry to Zion the news of 'the vengeance of the LORD... for his temple'; her young men fall in the streets. God avenges His house. The desecration of His worship is repaid in full.
Fugitives carry to Zion the news of 'the vengeance of the LORD... for his temple'; her young men fall in the streets. God avenges His house. The desecration of His worship is repaid in full.
'Behold, I am against thee, O thou most proud'; the proud shall stumble and fall, with none to raise him. God resists the haughty. Pride that defies heaven finds no helper in the day of its fall.
'Behold, I am against thee, O thou most proud'; the proud shall stumble and fall, with none to raise him. God resists the haughty. Pride that defies heaven finds no helper in the day of its fall.

Israel and Judah were oppressed and held fast, but 'their Redeemer is strong; the LORD of hosts... shall thoroughly plead their cause.' He takes their side. The strong Redeemer never abandons the cause of His captive people.

Israel and Judah were oppressed and held fast, but 'their Redeemer is strong; the LORD of hosts... shall thoroughly plead their cause.' He takes their side. The strong Redeemer never abandons the cause of His captive people.
A sword is upon the Chaldeans, on her princes, wise men, treasures, and waters, 'for it is the land of graven images.' Idolatry brings the sword. What a nation worships, by that it falls.
A sword is upon the Chaldeans, on her princes, wise men, treasures, and waters, 'for it is the land of graven images.' Idolatry brings the sword. What a nation worships, by that it falls.
A sword is upon the Chaldeans, on her princes, wise men, treasures, and waters, 'for it is the land of graven images.' Idolatry brings the sword. What a nation worships, by that it falls.
A sword is upon the Chaldeans, on her princes, wise men, treasures, and waters, 'for it is the land of graven images.' Idolatry brings the sword. What a nation worships, by that it falls.
Wild beasts shall haunt her; as God overthrew Sodom, none shall dwell in Babylon for ever. Utter desolation. The city that seemed eternal is wiped from the earth.
Wild beasts shall haunt her; as God overthrew Sodom, none shall dwell in Babylon for ever. Utter desolation. The city that seemed eternal is wiped from the earth.
A great people comes from the north; the king of Babylon hears the report, and his hands grow feeble with anguish. Terror seizes the mighty. The proud heart melts when God's judgment draws near.
A great people comes from the north; the king of Babylon hears the report, and his hands grow feeble with anguish. Terror seizes the mighty. The proud heart melts when God's judgment draws near.
A great people comes from the north; the king of Babylon hears the report, and his hands grow feeble with anguish. Terror seizes the mighty. The proud heart melts when God's judgment draws near.

Like a lion God comes up against her, asking, 'who is like me? and who will appoint me the time?' at the cry the earth trembles. His sovereignty is supreme. None can withstand or summon the Judge of all the earth.

Like a lion God comes up against her, asking, 'who is like me? and who will appoint me the time?' at the cry the earth trembles. His sovereignty is supreme. None can withstand or summon the Judge of all the earth.
